action.reasoning: Observation: Previous eval gave mIoU=0.0007 (essentially zero). Diagnose.py showed the model predicts 133 unique classes per image with pixel accuracy ~0.005 - essentially random. The nvidia/mit-b5 model should give ~47-50% mIoU on ADE20K. Analysis: The most likely issue is that the pre-downloaded model checkpoint doesn't include the fine-tuned classification head weights, or there's a config mismatch. The task description says to use AutoModel.from_pretrained('nvidia/mit-b5'), which might load only the backbone, not the full segmentation model. Decision: Run a comprehensive diagnostic to check the model config, classifier weights, and inference results. Then fix predict.py based on findings.
